items.title: Malaysian charged over 5kg methamphetamine seizure in Sydney Airport
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australian police have charged a Malaysian over an alleged attempt to import methamphetamine that was found concealed in luggage in Sydney Airport, reported Xinhua. The Australian Federal Police (AFP) and Australian Border Force (ABF) said in a joint statement on Friday that the 66-year-old man was sent for a baggage examination upon arrival in Sydney on a flight from Thailand on Thursday. An x-ray of the man's suitcase identified anomalies and a subsequent search located 10 bottles, which were labelled as scented powder, containing 5kg of methamphetamine.

items.title: ‘This is not the time for division,’ Anwar says as Malaysia grapples with oil price shock
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Malaysia’s economy has remained stable despite a sharp global surge in oil prices, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, assuring the public that the government is taking measures to cushion the impact on consumers. “Previously, prices were manageable. Now, with oil and gas installations being attacked and supply routes disrupted, prices have surged sharply,” Anwar said during a community gathering in Permatang Pauh.

items.title: #NSTViral: 'Moo-ve over': Buffaloes stray onto highway fast lane [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An adult buffalo and its calf wandering onto a busy highway have sparked safety concerns, as questions grow over how the animals ended up on the road. A female voice from inside a stationary vehicle can be heard expressing alarm as traffic moves past. Another female voice urges the driver to remain still, allowing the animals to pass safely.

items.title: RTD strengthens advocacy in road safety campaign for Aidilfitri
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Road Transport Department is intensifying the implementation of a road safety campaign in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri with a more strategic, comprehensive and high-impact approach to cultivate prudent driving among the public. Its director-general, Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli, said the campaign this year presents a new narrative that emphasises the delivery of safety messages in a more aggressive, creative, and inclusive manner through various platforms, including mainstream and digital media. He said the campaign is also strengthened through a strategic collaboration with well-known celebrities and influencers such as Datuk Sri Siti Nurhaliza, Zizan Razak, Izzue Islam and Boss James, who helped convey the safety message to various layers of society.

items.title: Police arrest man after 15km Myvi chase ends in crash
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police have arrested a local man in his 20s after a 15km high-speed chase along the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE) ended in a crash on Wednesday. Petaling Jaya police chief, Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat, said the incident occurred at 2.10pm when a patrol car attempted to stop a blue Perodua Myvi for inspection, but the driver sped off instead. "The suspect drove aggressively and attempted to evade police, triggering a pursuit that stretched 15 kilometres towards Klang," he said in a statement.

items.title: 12,000 litres of coconut milk sold out in five hours
items.summary: Trader Wan Yusof Wan Muda, 70, said the 12,000 litres were produced from 20 tonnes of old coconuts starting early this morning, with crowds forming as early as 6am. "We secured 20,000 old coconuts, or about 20 tonnes, from suppliers in several states, including Perak, last week. "We received 20,000 old coconuts last Sunday.

items.title: Gold set for third weekly fall on hawkish US Fed, elevated dollar
items.summary: BENGALURU: Gold prices edged up on Friday on technical buying, but were headed for a third consecutive weekly decline, pressured by a firm US dollar and as a hawkish US Federal Reserve dampened hopes for near-term interest rate cuts. Spot gold rose 0.6 per cent to US$4,675.23 per ounce as of 0702 GMT, rebounding from a near two-month low hit in the previous session. US gold futures for April delivery rose 1.6 per cent to US$4,676.90.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia increasing border surveillance amid Middle East turmoil
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today that Malaysia will heighten its border surveillance in response to growing turmoil and conflict in the Middle East. The prime minister said that maintaining internal stability is crucial to weathering external threats, warning that even minor domestic disputes must be handled decisively to prevent escalation. “Even small quarrels must be addressed immediately.

items.title: Smugglers who caused patrol boat to run aground linked to human trafficking syndicate
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The suspected smugglers who caused a marine patrol boat to run aground on a riverbank in Tumpat yesterday were believed to be members of a human trafficking syndicate operating from Tak Bai, Thailand. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said this was based on information provided by marine policemen involved in the operation. "They were travelling along Sungai Kelantan towards the riverbank at Kampung Teluk Renjuna when the incident occurred.

items.title: Crown Princess Mette-Marit reveals she was 'manipulated' by Jeffrey Epstein
items.summary: OSLO: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit said she was "manipulated" by convicted US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, in an interview broadcast Friday in which she sought to explain their close relationship. A commoner who married Crown Prince Haakon in 2001, Mette-Marit's name appears in new Epstein documents released by the US Department of Justice earlier this year. Mette-Marit has previously said that she deeply regretted her relationship with the disgraced financier.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Safeguard harmony, do not let small disputes spiral into chaos
items.summary: PERMATANG PAUH: The beauty of Malaysia lies in its people of diverse races and religions living together in harmony, and this unity must be preserved by avoiding infighting that could lead to chaos, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. "Malaysia is peaceful today, and we must ensure it remains so. Even small disputes must be handled quickly.

items.title: Three dead, five injured in road crash in Sri Aman
items.summary: KUCHING: Three people were killed and five others injured in an accident involving two vehicles near Kampung Selepong Jinggau, Sri Aman, about 8.30am today. "The three victims in the Proton X70 comprising a 70-year-old man and two women, both aged 22, were confirmed dead at the scene by the medical team. Meanwhile, three victims in the Perodua Alza, comprising an adult woman and two boys, managed to escape with minor injuries before the vehicle burst into flames and the victims were taken to hospital for treatment.

items.title: Thunderstorms expected in four states until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds in Penang, Perak, Pahang, and Sarawak until 5pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement that the affected areas in Penang are North Seberang Perai, Central Seberang Perai, and South Seberang Perai, while in Pahang, the warning covered Cameron Highlands and Lipis. The areas in Perak include Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, and Batang Padang.

items.title: PKR slams Hamzah's statement, says targeted diesel subsidy needs to go on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin's statement on the diesel subsidy issue does not reflect the overall current economic facts and has the potential to mislead the people, especially when the government is implementing the restructuring of fuel subsidies. PKR deputy secretary-general Sivamalar Genapathy said the statement repeats an old narrative that was emotional without taking into account the need for subsidy reform to ensure the country's fiscal sustainability. She said the previous subsidy system was exposed to serious leakages that cost billions of ringgit every year.

items.title: PM observes last day of Ramadan with Penang community ahead of Aidilfitri tomorrow
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim began his one-day working visit to Penang today by performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin in Kampung Pelet here. He arrived at the mosque at 1.12 pm and was received by Permatang Pauh UMNO division chief Datuk Mohd Zaidi Mohd Said, mosque chairman Othman Syafie and congregants. Anwar, together with nearly 700 worshippers, including Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid, then listened to a sermon titled “Ramadan, Ketika Senja Terakhir Berlabuh” delivered by Imam I Muhammad Nasir Hamid.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Govt closely monitors security, economic pressures
items.summary: PERMATANG PAUH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government had stepped up monitoring of both national security and economic risks as global tensions intensified, warning that overseas conflicts were increasingly affecting Malaysia directly. Speaking at a get-together with the local leader and people of Permatang Pauh here today, Anwar said the police, the armed forces and relevant agencies had been placed on heightened alert following the recent National Security Council meeting. "Oil prices have soared.

items.title: Sri Lanka refused ground access to US warplanes
items.summary: COLOMBO: Sri Lanka refused permission to the United States to station two of its warplanes at an airport in the island's south in early March, President Anura Kumara Dissanayake told parliament on Friday. "They wanted to bring two warplanes armed with eight anti-ship missiles from a base in Djibouti to the Mattala International Airport from March 4 to 8 and we said 'no'," Dissanayake said. Sri Lanka maintains close ties with the United States, its biggest export market, and Iran which is a key buyer of tea, its main export commodity.-AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RTS Link to boost Johor Baru-Singapore connectivity
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link project connecting Johor Baru and Singapore is being developed as part of a broader integrated mobility network linking the cross-border rail service with existing and future public transport systems in the city. Mass Rapid Transit Corp Sdn Bhd (MRT Corp) chief executive officer Mohd Zarif Hashim said integration had been a key guiding principle in the planning of the RTS Link from the outset. To realise this integrated transport vision, Mohd Zarif said MRT Corp had been working closely with the Transport Ministry, the Johor state government, Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ), the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), as well as the management of Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) to develop Bukit Chagar into a fully integrated transport hub.

items.title: US agency deepens probe into Tesla self-driving technology
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States (US) road safety authority said it was deepening its investigation into Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system over concerns about its performance in poor visibility, reported German Press Agency (dpa). Tesla chief Elon Musk has pushed for vehicles to operate autonomously using only cameras as sensors. If Musk's approach proves successful, Tesla could gain a significant cost advantage.

items.title: Anwar joins congregants for Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin
items.summary: PERMATANG PAUH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today joined the local community for Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin in Kampung Pelet. He arrived at 1.15pm and was received by Permatang Pauh Umno division chief Datuk Mohd Zaidi Mohd Said and Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid. After the prayers, congregants took the opportunity to greet and take selfies with the prime minister.

items.title: Malaysia's unsubsidised fuel price increase reflects global trends – Analyst
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Recent increases in unsubsidised fuel prices in Malaysia reflect rising global oil prices, according to an analyst. Geostrategist and Senior Fellow at the Nusantara Academy of Strategic Research (NASR), Prof Dr Azmi Hassan said the increase in global oil prices from US$60-US$65 per barrel previously to around US$110 currently has made price adjustments unavoidable. He said while the public generally understands that fuel prices have risen due to higher global oil prices, there remains a perception that prices should not rise as Malaysia is an oil-producing country.

items.title: Sabah Electricity ensures minimal power disruptions during Aidilfitri
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah utility authorities have ensured minimal power disruptions during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebration. Sabah Electricity chief executive officer Datuk Mohd Yaakob Jaafar said among the measures taken is the deployment of 500 personnel to remain on duty throughout the festive period. The workforce, comprising engineers, technicians and operations staff, was deployed across key facilities, including power stations, substations and system control centres statewide.

items.title: Diesel drums, tyre pile destroyed in Sabah fire
items.summary: KIMANIS: A fire involving diesel drums and a tyre pile at Batu 30, Kimanis, was brought under control after an overnight operation by the Fire and Rescue Department. According to the operations commander, the fire involved six diesel drums, three of which contained fuel. Of the affected units, one drum contained 18,000 litres of diesel, another 10,000 litres, and a third about 1,000 litres.

items.title: Happiness is built through care, solidarity and purpose
items.summary: Today, as the world marks the International Day of Happiness, we are reminded that happiness is not merely an individual pursuit but a shared global aspiration that must be nurtured through compassion, justice, and collective responsibility. In such a climate, the meaning of happiness must be redefined. To achieve happiness in today's context, several key principles must guide us: First, we must prioritise mental health and emotional well-being.

items.title: Fire at Kuwait refinery as Gulf states come under drone, missile attacks
items.summary: KUWAIT CITY: Drone attacks caused fire at Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery on Friday, state media reported, as authorities in several Gulf states said they were responding to attacks from Iran. The official Kuwait News Agency, citing the national oil company, said "several hostile drone attacks" hit the Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery, causing fires but no casualties. In Kuwait, an army statement said air defences were "responding to hostile missile and drone threats", while the UAE state media reported "incoming missile and drone threats from Iran."

items.title: Don't panic buy, Mat Sabu tells Malaysians amid global strains [WATCH]
items.summary: Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said his ministry, together with relevant agencies, is monitoring stock levels, prices and supply chains on a daily basis. He said swift action would be taken if any disruptions were detected. Also, support local products," he said in his Hari Raya Aidilfitri address.

items.title: M40 yet to feel full benefits of economic growth, says BNM governor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Malaysia’s middle-income (M40) group has yet to fully benefit from economic growth, with many still facing cost of living pressures, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said. According to a report by Berita Harian , he said stagnant wages among the group require labour market reforms to create higher-value jobs with better pay. “The government’s main focus is to create high-value jobs that can offer better wages,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: Sabah eyes China tourism boost with new Chongqing route
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is looking to boost international arrivals with the launch of a new direct flight from Chongqing, China. The inaugural Chongqing Airlines flight, carrying 154 passengers, landed at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) at 11.15pm yesterday. China Southern Airlines Kuala Lumpur office general manager Guo Hao said the new Chongqing–Kota Kinabalu route would strengthen connectivity between the two cities.

items.title: JB Southkey murder: Five suspects arrested, four nabbed in Singapore with SPF’s help
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 20 — Five foreign men have been arrested for their alleged involvement in the murder of a man in the Southkey commercial area here last week. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that following the murder, investigators from the state’s Serious Crimes Unit (D9) moved quickly to identify the suspects. Ab Rahaman said the first suspect apprehended in Johor has been remanded until March 24, while the four arrested in Singapore are remanded until March 26 to assist with the investigation.

items.title: Guan Eng mocks Penang CM over land tax, says 90pc revisions show system is broken
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng has demanded accountability from the state government after land tax bills were revised downward by as much as 90 per cent following errors by the Land and Mines Office. “If the land tax rate increase calculation system was indeed correct, fair, orderly and professional, what need would there be to reduce land tax by 80 per cent or 90 per cent?” he said. Lim cited additional cases of extreme tax increases affecting ordinary residents, including a kampung house in Juru whose land tax rose 2,842 times from RM12 to RM34,118, and a 57-year-old man whose ancestral land tax surged 727 times from RM29 to RM21,103.

items.title: Man arrested for recruiting schoolchildren into gangs in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Police have arrested a man suspected of recruiting school students to join gangs in the district. Seremban police chief Assistant Commissioner Azahar Abdul Rahim said the suspect has a prior criminal record involving four offences. Azahar said police had earlier received a report on a dispute involving Year Six students in Seremban on March 12.

items.title: Motorcyclist assaulted with baseball bat in Kulai after ‘terawih’ prayers
items.summary: The incident, which occurred at 9.50pm, allegedly stemmed from a road altercation where the victim was accused of cutting into the assailants’ car. Kulai district police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee confirmed that the victim had lodged a report and that police were aware of a video of the incident circulating online that matches the report. “The car was then driven recklessly before it intercepted the victim, forcing him to stop,” Tan explained.

items.title: Raya spike: Homes warned on power, water use
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The public has been urged to use electricity and water prudently during the Hari Raya Aidilfitri period, when consumption of both resources typically rises. The energy transition and water transformation minister said water supply operators are on alert to ensure demand is met, while the National Water Services Commission (Spad) has prepared contingency plans for any unexpected disruptions. The Energy Transition and Water Transformation Ministry (Petra), through Span, is targeting a maximum daily per capita water usage of 160 litres, in line with international benchmarks.

items.title: Diesel supply in Johor restored, monitoring continues
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Diesel supply across Johor has largely stabilised, with most petrol stations now back to normal operations following recent disruptions. She said diesel deliveries were now being carried out in stages to ensure all affected stations receive adequate supply. "The latest updates show that nearly all petrol stations in the affected areas have resumed normal diesel supply," she said.

items.title: Five foreigners arrested, four in Singapore, over Southkey murder
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have arrested five foreign men to assist in the investigation into the murder of a man in Southkey on March 14. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the 32-year-old foreign victim was attacked with a sharp weapon about 5am and was later confirmed dead at Sultan Ismail Hospital due to a stab wound to the neck. "Four more suspects were arrested at about 6pm on March 19 with the assistance of the Singapore police," he said in a statement.

items.title: Thunderstorm, heavy rain alert for Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Selangor, Perak and Sarawak until 3pm today. In a statement issued at noon, MetMalaysia said the area expected to be affected in Selangor is Sabak Bernam, while in Perak, the areas are Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak. In Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu (Sibu) and Mukah (Daro, Matu and Dalat).

items.title: Labour market reforms key to address stagnant M40 wages
items.summary: Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said although the economy is growing, the benefits may not yet be fully felt by the M40 group. He said a comprehensive solution to the issue lies in labour market reforms, with the government focusing on creating high-value jobs that offer better pay. Abdul Rasheed said creating more high-quality jobs would encourage the entire economy to move in tandem, adding that wage increases in one sector would create positive pressure for others to follow suit.

items.title: Three die after Proton X70 collides with Alza on Pan Borneo Highway
items.summary: KUCHING, March 20 — Three people were reportedly killed and five others injured in a crash involving two vehicles on the Pan Borneo Highway along the Serian–Sri Aman route near Kampung Selepong Jinggau today. “Two boys in the same vehicle sustained head injuries and were given initial treatment by the Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team before being sent to Sri Aman Hospital,” a JBPM spokesperson said. JBPM said the three victims who died were travelling in the Proton X70 — Lau Sie Tung, 70, and two women, Grace Lau Chi Ai, 22, and Lau Yok Leh, 22.

items.title: PLUS expects heavy traffic until night as festive travel peaks ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad reportedly expects traffic on the country’s major highways to remain heavy until late tonight as people travel back to their hometowns for Aidilfitri. Its senior general manager of operations Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz said daily traffic still exceeded two million vehicles despite a slight drop from the previous day, according to a report in Berita Harian. He reportedly said that accidents have been handled quickly through cooperation between PLUS, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Road Transport Department (JPJ).

items.title: Melaka food delivery rider remanded over violent attack on pregnant wife
items.summary: MELAKA, March 20 — A man who beat his pregnant wife on Sunday has been remanded for four days at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here, starting today. Yesterday, the police arrested a man who was accused of beating his pregnant wife, in a house in Taman Seri Kemendor, Jasin. Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit was reported to have said that the 22-year-old suspect was arrested by a team of police officers from the D8 and D11 units, Criminal Investigation Department (CID), Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) at around 11 am.

items.title: In Aidilfitri greetings, MCCBCHST calls for national unity and social cohesion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 – The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has extended its greetings to the Muslim community as the nation prepares to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on March 21. In a statement released today, the interfaith council described the festival as a significant time for reflection, gratitude, and renewal. MCCBCHST also said that racial and religious harmony remains the bedrock of Malaysia’s peace, stability, and ongoing progress.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert for several districts in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until noon today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said that in Sabah, the areas expected to be affected are Tawau (Tawau and Semporna), while in Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu and Dalat). — Bernama

items.title: Scammers use fake KL traffic police letterhead to solicit Raya donations, public warned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) has denied issuing an invitation letter for a Hari Raya Aidilfitri event that also asked for donations from the public. Kuala Lumpur JSPT chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa in a statement today said that his department detected the distribution of the fake letter via WhatsApp application at 5.50 pm yesterday. “Any confirmation can be made directly with the Kuala Lumpur JSPT at 03-2071 9999, the Kuala Lumpur JSPT Hotline at 03-2026 0267/ 0269 or any nearby police station,” he said.

items.title: Raya exodus: LLM says most routes under control except two key highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Traffic flow on several major highways was slow this morning as people began returning to their hometowns to celebrate Aidilfitri tomorrow. Congestion has also been detected on the KL-Karak Expressway heading east, with slow-moving traffic stretching 9.8 kilometres (km) from KM48.1 to KM57.9 (Bukit Tinggi). However, traffic conditions at the location remain manageable.

items.title: Government backbenchers hail 50pc toll discount, Raya aid measures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The MADANI Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) has welcomed and expressed full support for the government’s immediate measures to safeguard the well-being of the people ahead of the upcoming Hari Raya Aidilfitri. BBC chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said the approach taken by the government reflects a structured and targeted intervention framework, not only in easing the cost of living but also in strengthening the momentum of the people’s economic recovery. She said the approach encompasses key aspects, including cost of living, social welfare and market stability.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang’s Raya message: Be patient, obey traffic rules, arrive safely
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 20 — The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, has advised those driving back to their hometowns for Hari Raya Aidilfitri to always be patient and exercise caution when on the road. His Royal Highness, in a post on the Pahang Sultanate Facebook page yesterday, called on road users to always prioritise safety when driving, and to plan their journey properly to avoid stress and fatigue during long trips. “I urge all road users to be patient, obey traffic regulations and ensure your vehicles are in good condition before starting your journey.

items.title: Gua Musang police report over 30 minor accidents as Raya exodus begins
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 20 — More than 30 minor accidents have been reported along the Gua Musang route since yesterday, with most incidents believed to have been due to fatigue from driving for too long and too far. Gua Musang police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said most of the crashes occurred along the Kuala Lipis and Pahang-Gua Musang highways as well as Federal and state roads in the district. “However, there were no fatal accidents,” he told reporters after the Gua Musang-level Ops Selamat 26 Programme and the breaking of fast event at the Mentara Road Transport Department (JPJ) Enforcement Station here yesterday.

items.title: Aaron Ago Dagang, fellow ministers call for unity and compassion this Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Several Cabinet ministers have extended Aidilfitri greetings, emphasising the importance of unity, family values and moderation in Malaysia’s multiracial society. National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang, in a Facebook post tonight, said Aidilfitri is an ideal platform to strengthen family ties and foster goodwill among people of different faiths. Meanwhile, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said Aidilfitri serves as a reminder to return to core values such as family, compassion and togetherness.

items.title: MCMC warns iPhone users to update iOS now following ‘Darksword’ exploit discovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has urged Apple users to immediately update their iOS devices following the discovery of multiple security vulnerabilities. In a statement yesterday, MCMC said Apple had released an important security update addressing vulnerabilities, including an exploit chain known as “DarkSword” affecting iOS devices, particularly Apple iPhones. It said the vulnerabilities could be exploited together to compromise affected devices, potentially allowing unauthorised access and other malicious activities.

items.title: Police warn public after PD teen suffers ruptured eardrums, broken nose from modified firecrackers
items.summary: SEREMBAN, March 20 — A firecracker mishap last week left a 17-year-old boy with serious injuries, including ruptured eardrums in both ears and a broken nose in Bandar Springhill, Lukut in Port Dickson. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the teenager also suffered injuries to his fingers and palms, as well as minor wounds on his face. “The Port Dickson district police headquarters received a report on the incident today at 6.17 pm.

items.title: Indian snacks ‘muruku’, ‘kacang putih’ become Hari Raya favourites
items.summary: IPOH, March 20 —Traditional Indian snacks like muruku and kacang putih are becoming popular Hari Raya gifts, as many Malay customers fall in love with their spicy and savoury flavours. While Malays continue to make their traditional kuih and festive treats for Hari Raya, muruku and kacang putih have emerged as must-have snacks during the celebrations. Roshileh Osman, 57, from Lenggong, Gerik, said this was her first time buying kacang putih in Buntong, although she had previously purchased similar snacks in her hometown.

items.title: The sky is not the limit, it’s just the beginning for these women in aviation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The aviation sector has long been a male-dominated industry but women are boldly breaking through those barriers, making sacrifices to pursue their dreams, proving that passion and determination know no gender. It was reported that in 2021, women comprised 4.7 per cent of aircraft pilots globally, 3.1 per cent of aircraft maintenance engineers and 21.1 per cent of air traffic controllers, while in 2022, only six per cent of airline chief executive officers were women. Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG) said that as of March 6, 2026, women make up 36 per cent of total employees at the company.

items.title: Malaysia to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on Saturday
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — Muslims in Malaysia will celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on Saturday, March 21 this year. The announcement was made by Keeper of the Rulers’ Seal Tan Sri Syed Danial Syed Ahmad in a televised broadcast this evening. The announcement followed new moon sightings tonight at 29 locations nationwide.

items.title: Anwar congratulates Thailand’s Anutin on re-election, vows stronger bilateral ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Thailand’s Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on securing a renewed mandate to lead the country. In a Facebook post today, Anwar described Anutin as a “dear friend” and expressed his hopes for continued close engagement between the two leaders. According to international media reports, Anutin was re-elected prime minister today after securing a parliamentary majority.

items.title: Anwar hails NGO-led Malay-Indian reconciliation as sign of national maturity
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim described the reconciliation efforts being driven by non-governmental organisations (NGOs) representing the Malay and Indian communities as a reflection of the people’s maturity in choosing the path of peace and rejecting discord. He welcomed the reconciliation efforts, saying the initiative also uplifted the spirit of mutual respect between the races. Anwar also expressed his appreciation to all parties, including the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), who made the effort a success.

items.title: Heavy congestion hits highways out of Klang Valley as Hari Raya travel peaks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Traffic flow on major highways leading out of the Klang Valley is heavily congested this evening as vehicle volume surges ahead of the Hari Raya Aidilfitri holidays. She said traffic is also slow from Bukit Lanjan to Rawang, Taiping to Bukit Merah, Jawi to Bandar Cassia, and from Juru to Bertam. Meanwhile, traffic towards the east coast is slow-moving from the Gombak Toll Plaza to Bukit Tinggi, with congestion on the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) from Cheneh to Chukai and around the Kuala Terengganu Toll Plaza.

items.title: Hong Kong national among four held in Damansara Heights ‘mini casino’ bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Four people, including a Hong Kong national, were arrested yesterday for allegedly operating an online gambling syndicate from a “mini casino” in the affluent Bukit Damansara neighbourhood. Kuala Lumpur police chief Commissioner Datuk Fadil Marsus said the three men and one woman, aged between 27 and 49, were detained during a raid conducted at 3.30 yesterday, according to Utusan Malaysia. The case is being investigated under Section 4(1)(g) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 for operating a common gaming house, Fadil added.

items.title: Housing and Local Govt Ministry targets zero sick projects by 2030 under Madani housing reform, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) is stepping up efforts to strengthen governance in the real estate sector through its Madani Housing Reform agenda, with a target of achieving zero sick projects by 2030. “This Housing Reform aims to strengthen governance in the housing sector to achieve zero sick projects by 2030, while ensuring more Malaysians can own safe and livable homes,” he said in a statement today. Nga added that the Integrated Housing Monitoring System (HIMS) has been enhanced to enable real-time monitoring of project progress, financial flows and developers’ licence status.

items.title: Nirvana Asia suspects Cambodian scam syndicates behind urn theft-extortion plot
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Bereavement care giant Nirvana Asia Group has suggested that Cambodian scam syndicates may be responsible for the recent, organised theft of urns from memorial parks. The statement was issued after police confirmed a case involving 30 urns stolen from a memorial park in Nilai had evolved into an extortion attempt. Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya said a suspect using a foreign WhatsApp number contacted the park's management to demand payment for the return of the cremated remains.

items.title: CM: Penang raises funding for mosque, surau development to RM25m this year
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — The Penang government, through the Penang Islamic Religious Council (MAINPP), has allocated RM25.34 million this year for the development of mosques and surau across the state to strengthen religious institutions as centres of worship, education and community unity. “In line with the Penang Islamic Religious Development Agenda 2030 (APAI2030), which is based on the Vision Penang2030 framework in the field of Islamic education, Penang now has 352 Islamic schools and 222 Al-Quran and Fardu Ain (KAFA) classes registered under the Penang Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JHEAIPP). Chow pledged that the state leadership remains committed to strengthening the development of Islamic affairs as part of an inclusive state development agenda.
